title: Microsoft and Uber slam on the brakes of AI
transcript: So you might have heard the talk about AI bubbles kind of coming back to the forefront and there's two big things that happened in the news just in case you didn't hear. Number one is that Microsoft has canceled all of its cloud use internally and then the second thing is that Uber blew through its entire AI budget in a few months because they had posted I think it was them. Someone had posted an internal leaderboard basically saying who's used the most tokens and they used like $500 million of tokens in 5 months or something like that. I don't know if those are the actual numbers. Some of that was leaks, some of that was allegedly Amazon. So don't quote me on any of that, but the two big things is that people are realizing that AI is too expensive. That's that's the >> [sighs] >> the unifying trend here is that the narrative has become that Microsoft is canceling cloud because it was too expensive and Uber found out the hard way and Amazon found out the hard way that AI is just too expensive to run. So this has caused a lot of people to say, you know, "Aha, look, finally the bubble is about to pop." And there's so many people out there saying, you know, "I'm just I'm just waiting for AI to go to zero just like Bitcoin. It's going to go to zero." And so what I want to do is kind of take a look at at that narrative and you know, give give credit where credit is due. Um because as many people will point out you don't have to have something go to zero just for it to be a bubble. Really like the simplest definition is that an asset is overvalued. That is the most simple definition of a bubble. It's not technically the correct definition of a bubble, but as far as colloquial use, then yeah, that's fine. If you have an asset that is dramatically overvalued, then it's a bubble. Now there's a prime example for this. So Tesla, not sorry, not Tesla, another Elon Musk company, SpaceX uh presently getting staged for an IPO, and it's going to be north of a trillion dollars, and a lot of people on the internet are like, "Oh, no, this is so overvalued. It's going to be a liquidity trap." And that sort of stuff. The thing is is that its price-to-earnings ratio and all those other fancy financial numbers make it look like it is priced more like Tesla or more like Anthropic. And the reason is because xAI is attached to SpaceX now. So, all these things are not being priced like a rocket company. They're not being put priced like a car company. They're being priced like an AI company. Now, does that does that justify the astronomical price-to-earnings ratio? Probably not. But, here's the other thing. Even if we take all of that at face value, even if we accept, say, yes, it is a bubble, things are dramatically overpriced, that doesn't mean AI is going to stop. Like just like let's just worst-case scenario, the bubble pops and stock prices crash next year, right? Everyone realizes that Anthropic is not worth a trillion dollars, it's worth maybe 20 billion, right? It goes from a mega cap to a medium cap company. Uh even if that happens, the AI build-out is still happening. Uh when when you have every Fortune 500 company CEO is all a head full on AI, and they know that AI is the most transformative technology like [clears throat] of the decade, excuse me, I'm still a little little bit under the water. Under the weather, not under the water. Um anyways, so, when every venture capitalist and every Fortune 500 CEO is all a head full on AI, the the performance of any particular company doesn't really matter. Now, yes, you might say like, "Ah, well, if it does crash, Dave, then it's going to slow down a little bit." There's always going to be some friction, right? Uh as of this year, something like $150 billion worth of data centers has been delayed or canceled, which sounds like a lot until you realize that this year they're aiming for like $750 billion worth of data center build-outs. So, it's like a quarter, less than a quarter of data centers have been delayed. So, every time you see someone saying like, "Ah, you know, Michigan or Illinois or whoever, they got this data center canceled. Hooray, you know, and there was much rejoicing." That doesn't matter in the grand scheme of things. It's a drop in the bucket. Now, that doesn't mean that it's not nothing. It doesn't mean that that's zero resistance. But, it also needs to be taken in context. And so, when you have, you know, uh Microsoft cancels cloud. And by the way, Microsoft is still all in on AI. They're building their own tools. So, you know, take that for what it's worth. That's my view on the on the bubble talk. You know, when you when you look at the at the bigger picture, I don't see any any major reason for it to be slowing down. When venture capitalist start saying, "We were wrong about AI." or "It's not that exciting." or "We're moving on to something else." then maybe, you know, AI could be going to zero. But, let's be real, AI is not going to zero. So, with that being said, um I mentioned in my last video that my channel got demonetized, and a lot of you asked why. So, I figure um I had posted a couple times, but I'll just tell y'all uh the whole story. And it's really short, and it's really stupid, and it's at least half my fault. >> [snorts] >> So, some of you know that I had other channels. Well, if you if you get a channel monetized, then it's part of the YouTube Partner Program. And I started neglecting those other channels, and um one of them was dropped from the Partner Program. Um there's all kinds of reasons that it can get dropped. Mostly it had just stopped producing. Um the view counts and everything got too low, which meant that um it was suspended from the YouTube Partner Program. And YouTube, in their infinite wisdom, they view all suspensions as the same. So, [snorts] if you get suspended for uh policy violations, if you get suspended for copyright infringement, if you get suspended just for being inactive, it's a black mark on everything. Now, also YouTube, in their infinite wisdom, says that if one of your channels is suspended, all of your channels get suspended because now you're a risk. Now, this is That's the stupid part. The part that's my fault is that in trying to fix it, I deleted my other channels because I was like, "I'm not I'm not going to invest in these. I'm just going all in on my main channel." Which was a bad idea, because then it goes from a YPP suspension to a serious violation. Which means my main channel is now permanently and irrev- irrevocably demonetized forever for all time. I didn't [snorts] do anything wrong. And uh their appeals process was fully automated. Um I made an appeals video. No one watched it. >> [clears throat] >> It's sitting there on my channel unlisted with zero views. So, a human didn't even look at it. And uh so, that's where That's where uh That's where I'm at. But, I'm working on a bunch of other stuff. I've got Labor Zero, the book is coming out. Um we're aiming for late summer or fall. Uh of course, all these things always take longer than and think that they will, but we are working very hard on the book. Um then I've got my various communities. I've also got a new set of courses that I'm working on launching. It's on a platform called Kajabi. Um so stay tuned for that. Um I'll be teaching all kinds of stuff. And uh yeah, so thanks for asking and I'll check y'all next time. Take care.
